name: VGGT Scene Reconstruction
description: Feed-forward multi-view scene/room reconstruction (Meta VGGT-1B). Point it at a folder of overlapping photos of a room and it reconstructs the observed geometry — flat walls, real corners — then meshes it. Built for scenes, not single objects: do NOT remove backgrounds and provide several overlapping views. Outputs a colored mesh, and falls back to a point cloud if meshing is unavailable.
author: iammojogo

params_schema.label: Images Folder
params_schema.tooltip: Folder containing the overlapping photos of the room/scene. Every image in this folder is used. If left empty, only the single image wired into the node is used (poor for rooms).

params_schema.label: Reconstruction Mode

options.label: Joint — all images in one pass (default)
Incremental — one image at a time, then align

params_schema.tooltip: Joint runs all images through VGGT simultaneously and lets the model solve camera poses globally. Incremental runs VGGT on each image separately (better single-image quality) then stitches the clouds together using color correspondences. Try Incremental if joint produces petal/folding artifacts with your photos.

params_schema.label: Max Views
params_schema.tooltip: Caps how many photos are fed to the model. VGGT memory grows with view count; lower this if you hit out-of-memory.

params_schema.label: Confidence Filter
params_schema.tooltip: Drops the lowest-confidence depth points before meshing. Higher removes more noise and floaters but thins the cloud.

params_schema.label: Output
params_schema.tooltip: Mesh outputs a triangulated surface: Poisson if Open3D is installed, otherwise a dense per-view depth-grid mesh (no Open3D needed). Point cloud exports the raw colored points.

params_schema.label: Downsample
params_schema.tooltip: Voxel size for thinning the cloud before meshing. Larger = fewer points, faster, smoother.

params_schema.label: Mesh Detail
params_schema.tooltip: Poisson octree depth. Higher = more detail but more memory and more spurious surface.

params_schema.label: Trim Low Density
params_schema.tooltip: Removes the lowest-density Poisson vertices, trimming the inflated 'bubble' Poisson adds around sparse areas.

params_schema.label: Keep Largest Piece
params_schema.tooltip: Discards small disconnected mesh fragments, keeping only the largest connected surface.

params_schema.label: Planar Cleanup
params_schema.tooltip: Fits dominant flat planes (walls/floor/ceiling) to the point cloud. 'RANSAC planes' rebuilds each surface as a genuinely flat quad. 'Manhattan snap' also rotates them onto orthogonal axes so corners meet at true 90 degrees. Both replace the Poisson mesh; if no planes are found it exports the point cloud.

params_schema.label: Plane Tolerance
params_schema.tooltip: RANSAC inlier distance as a fraction of the scene size. Larger merges more points into each plane (good for noisy clouds); smaller keeps planes thin but may split a single wall in two.

params_schema.label: Max Planes
params_schema.tooltip: How many flat surfaces to extract, largest first. A bare room is ~6 (4 walls + floor + ceiling); raise it if furniture or shelves should also become planes.

params_schema.label: Grid Mesh Detail
params_schema.tooltip: Controls triangle count in the depth-grid mesh (used when Poisson/open3d is unavailable). Stride 1 = one vertex per depth pixel = maximum detail. Doubles faces for each step down.
